findings.description: The HTML converts the dense tabular layout into a more spaced-out table with significantly more vertical whitespace between rows, spreading content across more visual space than the original PDF.
findings.evidence: PDF shows compact rows fitting ~37 counties on one page; HTML shows the same data but with much larger row spacing, requiring more scrolling.

findings.description: The title 'FNS ACTIVE CASE ERROR RATES' and subtitle 'Oct 15 - Sept 16' have slightly different formatting in the HTML compared to the PDF centered bold header.
findings.evidence: PDF shows centered bold title with subtitle on same line; HTML shows left-aligned title and subtitle on separate lines.

findings.description: Table header row formatting differs slightly - the PDF has a more compact multi-line header with borders while the HTML has a cleaner styled header.
findings.evidence: PDF headers have visible cell borders and compact wrapping; HTML headers have more spacing and lighter styling.

findings.description: The STATE summary row at the bottom of the table is bold in the PDF but the bold emphasis appears preserved in the HTML.
findings.evidence: PDF shows bold 'STATE' row; HTML also shows bold STATE row.

findings.description: STATE AGENCY row is missing the # CORR CASE value. The PDF shows no explicit # CORR CASE value for STATE AGENCY but does show # COMP CASES = 3. The HTML correctly shows 3 for # COMP CASES but leaves # CORR CASE blank, which is consistent with the PDF.
findings.evidence: PDF STATE AGENCY row: 3 $963 [blank corr case] 1 $106 2 $197 3 $303 31.46% 68.54%. HTML matches this. No issue upon closer inspection.

findings.description: Alleghany (CO #3) row: PDF shows '0 $0' for # COMP CASES and TOTAL ISSUE. The HTML leaves these cells empty instead of showing 0 and $0.
findings.evidence: PDF: '3 ALLEGHANY 0 $0'. HTML: '3ALLEGHANY...' - missing the 0 and $0 values for COMP CASES and TOTAL ISSUE.

findings.description: Avery (CO #6) row: PDF shows '0 $0' for # COMP CASES and TOTAL ISSUE. The HTML leaves these cells empty.
findings.evidence: PDF: '6 AVERY 0 $0'. HTML has empty cells for COMP CASES and TOTAL ISSUE.

findings.description: Polk (CO #75) row: PDF shows '0 $0' for # COMP CASES and TOTAL ISSUE. The HTML leaves these cells empty.
findings.evidence: PDF: '75 POLK 0 $0'. HTML has empty cells.

findings.description: Tyrrell (CO #89) row: PDF shows '0 $0' for # COMP CASES and TOTAL ISSUE. The HTML leaves these cells empty.
findings.evidence: PDF: '89 TYRRELL 0 $0'. HTML has empty cells.

findings.description: Yadkin (CO #99) row: PDF shows '0 $0' for # COMP CASES and TOTAL ISSUE. The HTML leaves these cells empty.
findings.evidence: PDF: '99 YADKIN 0 $0'. HTML has empty cells.

findings.description: The HTML has a nested <main> inside <main>, and uses <h2> as the top-level heading for 'FNS ACTIVE CASE ERROR RATES' inside a header div. There is no <h1> used as the document title. The heading hierarchy is not well-structured.
findings.evidence:
HTML uses

FNS ACTIVE CASE ERROR RATES

inside a page-header div, with no h1 present. Document has nested
elements.

findings.description: The HTML has a nested <main> element which creates a structural duplication of the main content wrapper, though the actual table content is not duplicated.
